detailed_explanation: The time period from sunrise to around noon. Used for greetings and daily routines.
example_sentences: ["朝ごはんを食べる - Eat breakfast","朝は早い - The morning is early","毎朝 - Every morning"]
usage_notes: 朝ごはん means breakfast. Combined with other time words often.

detailed_explanation: The day after tomorrow. Common in scheduling and planning conversations.
example_sentences: ["明後日会いましょう - Let's meet the day after tomorrow","明後日は休み - Day after tomorrow is a day off","明後日までに - By the day after tomorrow"]
usage_notes: Use あさって in casual speech. More common than 明後日 in writing.

detailed_explanation: The day after today. Essential for making plans and appointments.
example_sentences: ["明日何をしますか - What will you do tomorrow?","明日は雨 - Tomorrow it will rain","明日までに - By tomorrow"]
usage_notes: Very common time word. Often combined with particles に or まで.

detailed_explanation: Describes hot weather or high temperature. For hot objects/food use 熱い (same reading, different kanji).
example_sentences: ["今日は暑い - It's hot today","暑い国 - Hot country","暑くなった - It became hot"]
usage_notes: Distinguish: 暑い = weather/temperature, 熱い = objects/food/fever.

detailed_explanation: Describes hot objects, food, or fever. Same pronunciation as 暑い but different meaning.
example_sentences: ["お茶が熱い - The tea is hot","熱いスープ - Hot soup","熱があります - Have a fever"]
usage_notes: Use for physical hot things and fever. Not for weather - use 暑い instead.

detailed_explanation: One's own older brother. Use お兄さん for other people's older brothers (polite).
example_sentences: ["兄がいる - I have an older brother","兄は大学生 - My older brother is a university student","兄と遊ぶ - Play with my older brother"]
usage_notes: Use 兄 for your own brother, お兄さん for others' brothers. Context determines if humble or neutral.

detailed_explanation: One's own older sister. Use お姉さん for other people's older sisters (polite).
example_sentences: ["姉は東京にいる - My older sister is in Tokyo","姉と話す - Talk with my older sister","姉の家 - Older sister's house"]
usage_notes: Use 姉 for your own sister, お姉さん for others' sisters. Shows humility when referring to family.

detailed_explanation: Physical house or one's home. Used for where someone lives or a building.
example_sentences: ["家に帰る - Go home","家が大きい - The house is big","うちの家 - My house"]
usage_notes: 家 is more formal than うち. うち implies 'my/our home' more intimately.

detailed_explanation: Basic movement verb. Motion away from speaker. Irregular in some forms (te-form: 行って).
example_sentences: ["学校に行く - Go to school","どこに行く - Where are you going?","行きましょう - Let's go"]
usage_notes: Direction particle に or へ often used. 行って is te-form (note double-t).

detailed_explanation: The number one. Also means 'best' or 'first' in compounds.
example_sentences: ["一つください - Please give me one","一番 - Number one/best","一人 - One person"]
usage_notes: Pronunciation changes in compounds: いち, いっ, ひと. Readings depend on counter used.

detailed_explanation: Question word for time. Used to ask when something happens or happened.
example_sentences: ["いつ来ますか - When will you come?","いつから - From when?","いつまで - Until when?"]
usage_notes: Also written as いつ in hiragana. Very common in questions. Often with から or まで.

detailed_explanation: Domestic dog. Common pet and animal word. Counter: 匹 (ひき).
example_sentences: ["犬が好き - I like dogs","犬を飼う - Keep a dog","犬の散歩 - Walk the dog"]
usage_notes: 犬 is general term. いぬ is less common reading. Honorific: いぬさん jokingly for pets.

detailed_explanation: Current time, the present. Also used to mean 'just now' or 'immediately' in compounds.
example_sentences: ["今忙しい - Busy now","今から - From now","今すぐ - Right now"]
usage_notes: Very common time word. 今から = from now on. 今まで = until now.

detailed_explanation: The sea or ocean. Also beach/seaside. Common in travel and nature contexts.
example_sentences: ["海に行く - Go to the sea","海が好き - I like the sea","海で泳ぐ - Swim in the sea"]
usage_notes: 海 often implies beach vacation in casual speech. Counter 面 (めん) for surface areas.

detailed_explanation: To sell something. Transitive verb (seller does the action).
example_sentences: ["車を売る - Sell a car","売りますか - Will you sell?","売店 - Shop/selling place"]
usage_notes: Opposite: 買う (buy). For something selling well: 売れる (potential/intransitive form).

detailed_explanation: Candy, snacks, confectionery. Polite term with お prefix. Small gift tradition in Japan.
example_sentences: ["お菓子を食べる - Eat sweets","お菓子屋 - Candy shop","お菓子を買う - Buy snacks"]
usage_notes: Common in omiyage (souvenir) culture. Many snack shops in Japan. Can be sweet or savory.

detailed_explanation: Currency, cash, funds. Essential for shopping and financial conversations.
example_sentences: ["お金がない - No money","お金を払う - Pay money","お金を貯める - Save money"]
usage_notes: Colloquial: かね (casual, sometimes vulgar). お金 is standard polite form.

detailed_explanation: Alcoholic beverages. Can mean Japanese sake specifically or alcohol generally.
example_sentences: ["お酒を飲む - Drink alcohol","お酒は好き - I like alcohol","お酒に弱い - Weak with alcohol"]
usage_notes: Sake specifically: 日本酒 (にほんしゅ). お酒 is general alcohol term. Social drinking common in Japan.

detailed_explanation: Tea (green tea, black tea, etc.). Also used for tea break or tea ceremony.
example_sentences: ["お茶を飲む - Drink tea","お茶を入れる - Make tea","お茶はどうですか - How about some tea?"]
usage_notes: Often green tea (日本茶). Tea break: お茶する (casual verb form). Social ritual in Japan.

detailed_explanation: Father. Use for your own father (casual) or others' fathers (polite).
example_sentences: ["お父さんは元気 - Father is well","お父さんに聞く - Ask father","お父さんの車 - Father's car"]
usage_notes: Other forms: 父 (ちち) humble for own father. 父さん (casual). パパ (kids language).

detailed_explanation: One's own younger brother. Use 弟さん for other people's younger brothers (polite).
example_sentences: ["弟がいる - I have a younger brother","弟と遊ぶ - Play with younger brother","弟は学生 - Younger brother is a student"]
usage_notes: 弟 for own, 弟さん for others'. おとうと is general reading. Age relative to speaker matters.

detailed_explanation: Mother. Use for your own mother (casual) or others' mothers (polite).
example_sentences: ["お母さんに電話する - Call mother","お母さんの料理 - Mother's cooking","お母さんは元気 - Mother is well"]
usage_notes: Other forms: 母 (はは) humble for own mother. 母さん (casual). ママ (kids language).

detailed_explanation: Someone else's older brother or addressing your own older brother respectfully.
example_sentences: ["お兄さんは何歳 - How old is (your) older brother?","お兄さんがいる - I have an older brother","田中さんのお兄さん - Tanaka's older brother"]
usage_notes: For own brother to outsiders: 兄. For addressing brother directly: お兄ちゃん or 兄さん.

detailed_explanation: Someone else's older sister or addressing your own older sister respectfully.
example_sentences: ["お姉さんは結婚している - (Your) older sister is married","お姉さんと買い物 - Shopping with older sister","お姉さんの車 - Older sister's car"]
usage_notes: For own sister to outsiders: 姉. For addressing sister directly: お姉ちゃん or 姉さん.

detailed_explanation: Casual/family way to say mother. Used when addressing or referring to mother.
example_sentences: ["母さんに聞く - Ask mother","母さんの料理 - Mom's cooking","母さん、ただいま - Mom, I'm home"]
usage_notes: Less formal than お母さん but not humble. Family casual speech. Not used for others' mothers.

detailed_explanation: To purchase something with money. Very common daily activity verb.
example_sentences: ["本を買う - Buy a book","買い物する - Do shopping","何を買う - What will you buy?"]
usage_notes: 買い物 (かいもの) = shopping. Note te-form: 買って (double small tsu).

detailed_explanation: To go back home or to one's origin place. Directional verb.
example_sentences: ["家に帰る - Go home","国に帰る - Return to country","帰りましょう - Let's go home"]
usage_notes: 帰る is specifically returning to a home/base. 戻る is returning to any place.

detailed_explanation: Umbrella for rain or sun. Essential item in rainy Japan.
example_sentences: ["傘をさす - Open/put up umbrella","傘がない - No umbrella","傘を忘れる - Forget umbrella"]
usage_notes: Rain umbrella: 雨傘 (あまがさ). Also カサ in katakana for Western-style. Lost umbrella common in Japan.

detailed_explanation: To lend something to someone. The lender is the subject.
example_sentences: ["本を貸す - Lend a book","お金を貸す - Lend money","貸してくれる - Lend to me (with gratitude)"]
usage_notes: Opposite: 借りる (borrow). 貸す = I lend (to you). 借りる = I borrow (from you).

detailed_explanation: Wind or a cold (illness). Context determines which meaning.
example_sentences: ["風が強い - Wind is strong","風をひく - Catch a cold","風が吹く - Wind blows"]
usage_notes: 風邪 (with 邪) specifically means cold/flu. 風 alone can be either. 風邪をひく = catch cold.

detailed_explanation: Family members, household. Refers to the family unit.
example_sentences: ["家族が好き - I love my family","家族で旅行 - Travel with family","大家族 - Big family"]
usage_notes: 家族 refers to family members. うち/いえ refers to house/home. 家族旅行 = family trip.

detailed_explanation: North direction. One of four cardinal directions. Used in addresses and navigation.
example_sentences: ["北に行く - Go north","北の方 - Northern direction","北海道 - Hokkaido (north sea road)"]
usage_notes: Part of many place names. 北側 (north side), 北口 (north exit common at stations).

detailed_explanation: This day, the current day. Essential for daily conversation.
example_sentences: ["今日は暑い - Today is hot","今日何日 - What day is today?","今日から - From today"]
usage_notes: Very common. 今日は (topic) often used at sentence start. 今日から = starting today.

detailed_explanation: Casual 'you', often used by males to equals or subordinates. Can also mean 'monarch/ruler'.
example_sentences: ["君は学生 - You are a student","君の名は - Your name is","君たち - You all"]
usage_notes: Mostly male speech. Females use あなた more often. 君 can sound blunt or intimate depending on context.

detailed_explanation: To put on/wear clothes on upper body (shirts, sweaters, etc.).
example_sentences: ["シャツを着る - Wear a shirt","何を着る - What will you wear?","着替える - Change clothes"]
usage_notes: Specific to upper body. 履く (はく) for lower body/footwear. かぶる for head. める for accessories.

detailed_explanation: To come, to arrive. Motion toward speaker's location. Irregular verb (来ない, 来て, 来た, 来よう).
example_sentences: ["学校に来る - Come to school","いつ来る - When will you come?","来てください - Please come"]
usage_notes: Very irregular: 来ない (not 来らない), 来て, 来た. Motion toward speaker or their place.

detailed_explanation: The morning of today. Used for events that happened earlier today.
example_sentences: ["今朝早く起きた - Woke up early this morning","今朝何を食べた - What did you eat this morning?","今朝から雨 - Rain since this morning"]
usage_notes: 今朝 = today morning. 明日の朝 = tomorrow morning. Contrast with 今晩 (this evening).

detailed_explanation: Entrance area of house/building where shoes are removed. Unique Japanese home feature.
example_sentences: ["玄関で靴を脱ぐ - Remove shoes at entrance","玄関が広い - Entrance is wide","玄関先 - In front of entrance"]
usage_notes: Shoes removed here, not worn past this point. Small step up to house floor level. Shoe cabinet often here.

detailed_explanation: This place, here (near speaker). One of the three location demonstratives.
example_sentences: ["ここにいる - I'm here","ここは静か - This place is quiet","ここで待つ - Wait here"]
usage_notes: こそあど words: ここ(here), そこ(there-near listener), あそこ(there-far), どこ(where). Often written in hiragana.

detailed_explanation: Polite form of ここ/これ/この人. Used for people or directions in formal contexts.
example_sentences: ["こちらこそ - The pleasure is mine","こちらへどうぞ - This way please","こちらは田中さん - This is Mr./Ms. Tanaka"]
usage_notes: こちら = polite これ. そちら = polite それ. あちら = polite あれ. Used for introductions and directions.

detailed_explanation: The current month. Used for scheduling and describing current time periods.
example_sentences: ["今月忙しい - Busy this month","今月の予定 - This month's schedule","今月いっぱい - Until the end of this month"]
usage_notes: Similar pattern: 今週 (this week), 今年 (this year). 先月 = last month, 来月 = next month.

detailed_explanation: The current week (Monday-Sunday typically). Common for scheduling.
example_sentences: ["今週何をする - What will you do this week?","今週は忙しい - This week is busy","今週末 - This weekend"]
usage_notes: 先週 = last week, 来週 = next week. Business weeks usually start Monday in Japan.

detailed_explanation: Fish (animal or food). Very important in Japanese cuisine and culture.
example_sentences: ["魚を食べる - Eat fish","魚が好き - Like fish","新鮮な魚 - Fresh fish"]
usage_notes: Essential in Japanese diet. Counter: 匹 (small fish) or 尾 (large fish). Raw: 刺身 (sashimi), cooked: 焼き魚.
